cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out between what times David could have gone to the football field. The problem gives several time intervals when David was seen doing different things, and we know the football field was closed after 10pm. So the answer must be a time when David wasn't doing any of the listed activities and before 10pm. First, let me list all the times David was accounted for: 1. David woke up at 5am. But waking up doesn't necessarily take up time, so maybe that's just a starting point. But Steven saw him at the electronics store from 5am to 6am. So between 5-6am, he's at the store. 2. Kimberly saw David taking photos at the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 6am to 8am. So that's 6-8am. 3. Betty saw him working out at the gym from 9am to 12pm. So 9-12. 4. Lisa saw him driving to the water park from 12pm to 3pm. 12-3pm. 5. William saw him on a rooftop from 3pm to 10pm. 3-10pm. So the football field was closed after 10pm, so he had to go before that. Let's check the timeline: From 5am to 6am: electronics store. 6am to 8am: Leaning Tower of Pisa. Then from 8am to 9am: Wait, between 8am and 9am, what was he doing? The next activity is gym at 9am. So between 8am and 9am, there's a gap. Let me check if there's any other activity. No, because Kimberly's sighting ends at 8am, and Betty's starts at 9am. So 8-9am is free. Then from 9am to 12pm: gym. 12pm to 3pm: driving to water park. 3pm to 10pm: on a rooftop. So the only time slot when David wasn't doing anything is 8am to 9am.
